groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Topic, goal, audience, promise, facts, format, language, duration, tone, voice, CTA.

groups.title: Story, Structure & Retention
groups.description: Angle, sections, opening, structure, pacing, retention, examples, questions.

groups.title: Channel Voice & Production
groups.description: Channel context, brand voice, host, visuals, B-roll, on-screen text, editing.

groups.title: Discovery & Publishing
groups.description: Title direction, keywords, description, chapters, pinned comment.

groups.title: Accuracy & Commercial Context
groups.description: Sources, commercial relationship, disclosures, regulated topics, restrictions.

variables.label: Video topic
variables.placeholder: Explain what the video will be about.
variables.hint: Describe the subject, event, product, question, story, or idea the video should cover.

help.why: The script cannot be created without knowing its central subject.
help.example: What the long-form video will cover
help.avoid: Several unrelated subjects in one video.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main goal
variables.hint: Choose the most important outcome the video should achieve.

help.why: The goal determines the script’s emphasis, structure, evidence, and CTA.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Example: First-time founders building small SaaS products
variables.hint: Describe who should watch and why the topic matters to them.

help.why: Audience context materially changes vocabulary, examples, pacing, and assumed knowledge.
help.example: Example: First-time founders building small SaaS products
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Main viewer promise
variables.placeholder: What should viewers understand, feel, or be able to do afterward?
variables.hint: State the central payoff the video must deliver.

help.why: A clear promise keeps the hook, body, and conclusion aligned.
help.example: What viewers should understand or be able to do afterward
help.avoid: Vague promises the video cannot deliver.

variables.label: Essential points and facts
variables.placeholder: List the facts, arguments, steps, names, examples, or details that must appear.
variables.hint: Include only information the final script must not omit.

help.why: The model must know which user-supplied information is mandatory.
help.example: Only mandatory facts, steps, or arguments
help.avoid: Invented stats or first-hand stories.

variables.label: Video format
variables.placeholder: Select a format
variables.hint: Choose the format that best matches how the topic should be presented.

help.why: Different YouTube formats require different narrative and production structures.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Russian, Spanish
variables.hint: The complete script and supporting assets will use this language.

help.why: Language cannot always be safely inferred from the topic or audience.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Target duration
variables.hint: Enter the approximate finished video length in minutes, normally between 3 and 60.

help.why: Duration determines script depth, word budget, number of sections, and pacing.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Audience knowledge level
variables.placeholder: Select a knowledge level
variables.hint: Controls how much background explanation and specialist terminology are appropriate.

help.why: The same subject requires different explanations for beginners and experienced viewers.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Tone and style
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the dominant delivery style without prescribing every sentence.

help.why: Tone affects word choice, rhythm, emotional intensity, and presenter delivery.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Narrator perspective
variables.placeholder: Select a perspective
variables.hint: Choose how the presenter or narrator should address the audience.

help.why: Perspective influences pronouns, intimacy, authority, and script formatting.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Primary call to action
variables.placeholder: Example: Subscribe, comment, download the guide, or visit the product page
variables.hint: Use one primary action. Leave as Auto for a restrained, goal-appropriate CTA.

help.why: The intended next action changes how the closing and earlier CTA setup are written.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Preferred angle
variables.placeholder: Example: Focus on mistakes beginners make rather than a general overview
variables.hint: Specify the viewpoint or framing that should make this video distinctive.

help.why: A specific angle prevents the script from becoming a generic topic summary.
help.example: Example: Focus on mistakes beginners make rather than a general overview
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Preferred sections
variables.placeholder: List sections, steps, arguments, or story stages in the preferred order.
variables.hint: Leave blank to let the model build the strongest logical progression.

help.why: Some creators already have a required sequence or editorial structure.
help.example: List sections, steps, arguments, or story stages in the preferred order.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Opening approach
variables.placeholder: Select an opening style
variables.hint: Controls how the script earns attention before providing background.

help.why: The first moments strongly affect the script’s framing and retention strategy.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Narrative structure
variables.placeholder: Select a structure
variables.hint: Choose a progression or let the model select one based on the format.

help.why: The structure determines how information and payoffs are sequenced.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Pacing
variables.placeholder: Select a pacing style
variables.hint: Controls section length, sentence rhythm, and frequency of visual changes.

help.why: Pacing must match the audience, subject, duration, and editing approach.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Retention techniques
variables.placeholder:
Auto
Open loops
Progress markers
Viewer questions
Mini-payoffs
Visual reveals
Pattern interrupts
Later callbacks
None
variables.hint: Choose techniques to use naturally, not as artificial engagement tricks. Select all that apply (one per line). Options: Auto; Open loops; Progress markers; Viewer questions; Mini-payoffs; Visual reveals; Pattern interrupts; Later callbacks; None.

help.why: It lets creators influence retention without asking them to design the entire script.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Examples or stories
variables.placeholder: Add real examples, personal experiences, scenarios, demonstrations, or analogies.
variables.hint: Only supplied experiences may be presented as real or first-hand.

help.why: Real examples can make abstract information understandable and credible.
help.example: Add real examples, personal experiences, scenarios, demonstrations, or analogies.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Questions to address
variables.placeholder: What doubts, objections, myths, or viewer questions should the script answer?
variables.hint: Use this for important concerns that should be resolved during the video.

help.why: Addressing genuine viewer uncertainty improves usefulness and narrative progression.
help.example: What doubts, objections, myths, or viewer questions should the script answer?
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Channel context
variables.placeholder: Describe the channel, recurring topics, host, audience relationship, or series context.
variables.hint: Include only context that should influence this specific script.

help.why: Channel context prevents the script from sounding disconnected from existing content.
help.example: Describe the channel, recurring topics, host, audience relationship, or series context.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Voice guidelines
variables.placeholder: Example: Informal, skeptical, dry humor, short sentences, no motivational language
variables.hint: Describe distinctive voice traits or provide a short style sample.

help.why: A creator’s established voice cannot be reliably inferred from the topic alone.
help.example: Example: Informal, skeptical, dry humor, short sentences, no motivational language
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Presenter format
variables.placeholder: Select a presenter format
variables.hint: Determines how spoken sections and production notes are balanced.

help.why: A face-to-camera script is written differently from a voice-over or screen-recorded tutorial.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Available visuals
variables.placeholder: List footage, screenshots, product shots, locations, interviews, charts, or assets available.
variables.hint: The script will not assume access to expensive or unavailable material.

help.why: Production directions should reflect resources the creator can actually use.
help.example: List footage, screenshots, product shots, locations, interviews, charts, or assets available.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Visual-note detail
variables.placeholder: Select the visual-note level
variables.hint: Controls how detailed the B-roll and scene directions should be.

help.why: Creators have different production workflows and levels of visual planning.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: On-screen text style
variables.placeholder: Select a text style
variables.hint: Controls callouts, captions, labels, and emphasis text in the production notes.

help.why: Text-heavy and visually minimal videos need different scripting and editing cues.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Editing intensity
variables.placeholder: Select an editing style
variables.hint: Sets the expected frequency of cuts, inserts, visual changes, and pauses.

help.why: The script’s sentence length and visual rhythm should match the intended editing style.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Title direction
variables.placeholder: Select a title style
variables.hint: Controls how the title balances clarity, curiosity, search intent, and viewer benefit.

help.why: Different channels and topics require different title positioning.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Primary search phrase
variables.placeholder: Example: how to validate a SaaS idea
variables.hint: Use only when a specific search phrase matters; natural wording takes priority.

help.why: A supplied search phrase can align the title, introduction, and description with viewer intent.
help.example: Example: how to validate a SaaS idea
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Supporting search terms
variables.placeholder: Separate a few related phrases with commas.
variables.hint: Use naturally where relevant; never force repetition or keyword stuffing.

help.why: Related terminology can improve topic coverage when the user has a defined search strategy.
help.example: Separate a few related phrases with commas.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: YouTube description
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include it
variables.hint: Auto includes a concise description when it adds practical value.

help.why: Some users need only the script, while others need a ready-to-upload description.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Video chapters
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include chapters
variables.hint: Auto adds chapters when the duration and structure make them useful.

help.why: Chapters are useful for structured long videos but unnecessary for every script.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Pinned comment
variables.placeholder: Choose whether to include one
variables.hint: Auto adds one only when it supports discussion, clarification, or the primary CTA.

help.why: A pinned comment can support a specific goal but should not be generated automatically for every video.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Sources and reference material
variables.placeholder: Paste verified notes, data, quotations, URLs, excerpts, specifications, or source summaries.
variables.hint: The script may use only the claims supported by this material or other supplied facts.

help.why: Source material is essential when factual precision, quotations, current information, or evidence matters.
help.example: Paste verified notes, data, quotations, URLs, excerpts, specifications, or source summaries.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Commercial relationship
variables.placeholder: Select any relevant relationship
variables.hint: Identify ownership, sponsorship, affiliate, or partnership context.

help.why: Commercial context affects disclosure, claims, CTA wording, and audience trust.
help.avoid: Hidden sponsorship or fake independent review framing.

variables.label: Offer or sponsor details
variables.placeholder: Describe the product, sponsor, value, approved claims, CTA, and placement requirements.
variables.hint: Provide only accurate information approved for use in the video.

help.why: Commercial messaging cannot be written accurately without the supplied offer and claim details.
help.example: Describe the product, sponsor, value, approved claims, CTA, and placement requirements.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Required disclosure
variables.placeholder: Add required sponsorship, affiliate, partnership, or jurisdiction-specific wording.
variables.hint: The model may improve readability but must preserve the meaning of required disclosures.

help.why: The creator or advertiser may have mandatory disclosure language the model cannot infer.
help.example: Add required sponsorship, affiliate, partnership, or jurisdiction-specific wording.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Sensitive or regulated topic
variables.placeholder: Select when special care is required
variables.hint: Use this for content where inaccurate claims could cause meaningful harm.

help.why: High-stakes subjects require cautious wording and different verification behavior.
help.avoid: Medical/legal claims without approved wording.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: Example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, California
variables.hint: Specify the country, state, or region when laws, rules, or public systems are discussed.

help.why: Legal, financial, political, tax, employment, and regulatory information may vary by jurisdiction.
help.example: Example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, California
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Restrictions and additional context
variables.placeholder: Add prohibited claims, sensitive details, pronunciation notes, links, names, or other requirements.
variables.hint: Use this single field for important instructions not covered elsewhere.

help.why: It captures meaningful exceptions without creating several miscellaneous fields.
help.example: Add prohibited claims, sensitive details, pronunciation notes, links, names, or other requirements.
help.avoid: Invented proof, fake urgency, fabricated stories, or unsupported claims.
